User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account within the LibreConnect Analytics application, detailing their authentication information, role, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's institutional email address, used for authentication.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The assigned role of the user (e.g., 'admin', 'visitor').

isBlocked.description: Indicates whether the user's access has been blocked by an administrator.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

Visit.title: Visit
Visit.description: Records a single check-in event by a visitor to the library, capturing details about their attendance and purpose.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Visit entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who performed this visit. (Relationship: User 1:N Visit)

checkInTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the visitor checked into the library.

collegeDepartment.description: The college department specified by the visitor during check-in.

reasonForVisit.description: The reason provided by the visitor for their visit to the library.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. This collection contains user metadata, including their 'role' (admin, visitor) for client-side logic and 'isBlocked' status for account management.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user.

definition.description: Records individual library check-in events. Each document includes the `userId` of the visitor, denormalizing ownership for authorization independence and enabling efficient global queries for analytics. Visitors can only create and read their own visit records, while admins can access all records.

params.name: visitId
params.description: A unique identifier for each library visit record.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for robust Firebase Security Rules, where the existence of a document at `/admins/{request.auth.uid}` signifies an administrator role. Documents in this collection typically contain minimal or no data; their primary purpose is for atomic role checks (`exists()`) in security rules.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of a user designated as an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Accurate Permissions (QAPs) by applying denormalization and structural segregation principles. 

1.  **Authorization Independence (via Denormalization):**
    *   **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** Each user's profile is stored in a document whose ID is their Firebase Authentication UID. This directly links the document to the authenticated user, making self-ownership checks (`request.auth.uid == userId`) atomic and independent, requiring no `get()` calls to other documents for basic user profile access.
    *   **Visit Records (`/visits/{visitId}`):** Every `Visit` document explicitly includes a `userId` field. This denormalizes the ownership information directly into the `Visit` record itself. Security rules can thus verify ownership (`resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`) without needing to fetch any parent or related user document. This design also facilitates atomic creation of visit records.
    *   **Admin Role (`/admins/{adminId}`):** A dedicated, top-level collection `/admins` is used where document IDs are the UIDs of administrator users. The *existence* of a document at `/admins/{request.auth.uid}` is the sole determinant of admin privileges in security rules. This strategy, prioritizing "Existence over Content," provides an extremely fast and atomic check for global admin roles, completely avoiding `get()` calls to potentially larger user profile documents (`/users/{userId}`) just to ascertain a role. The `role` field within the `/users/{userId}` document serves for client-side display and filtering, while `/admins/{adminId}` is for robust rule enforcement.

2.  **Query-Accurate Permissions (QAPs):**
    *   **Visitor Access:** For visitors to record their check-ins, they will write to the `/visits` collection. Rules will enforce that `resource.data.userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. When visitors view their own past visits, they can query `/visits` using `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. Since the `userId` field is present and enforced in every `Visit` document, security rules can precisely ensure that non-admin users only retrieve their own records, thereby satisfying QAPs for personal data retrieval.
    *   **Admin Access:**
        *   **Global Analytics and Management:** The `/visits` and `/users` collections are structured as top-level collections. This allows administrators, whose role is verified by the `exists(/admins/$(request.auth.uid))` check, to perform global `list` and `get` operations on all `Visit` and `User` documents without restriction. This design natively supports the requirements for real-time statistics (e.g., total visitors by day, week, month), user management (search, block user), and AI-powered trend analysis, as admin queries will naturally return all relevant data, fully satisfying QAPs for administrative functions.
        *   **Structural Segregation:** By having separate top-level collections for `users` and `visits` (and `admins` for roles), documents within each collection share a homogeneous security posture. This simplifies security rules, moving complexity from intricate logic to clear structural boundaries.
